1) to wait, wait for, await. 1a) (Qal) to wait for. 1b) (Piel): 1b1) to wait, tarry; 1b2) to wait (in ambush); 1b3) to wait for, long for.

1) to be weary, be impatient, be grieved, be offended. 1a) (Qal) to be weary, be impatient. 1b) (Niphal) to be tired of something, weary oneself. 1c) (Hiphil) to weary, make weary, exhaust.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: patience, forbearance, internal and external control in a difficult circumstance, which control could exhibit itself by delaying an action; patient enduring of evil, fortitude, Col. 1:11; Col. 3:12; 1Tim. 1:16; 1Pet. 3:20; slowness of avenging injuries, long-suffering, forbearance, clemency, Rom. 2:4; 9:22; 2Cor. 6:6; Gal. 5:22; Eph. 4:2; 2Tim. 4:2; Jas. 5:10; patient expectation, 2Tim. 3:10; Heb. 6:12; 2Pet. 3:15.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: makro-thumia, Ionic dialect -ie, he, long-suffering, patience, [NT+4th c.BC+]; 2 forbearance, [LXX+NT]

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to have patience; to be patient; to exhibit internal and external control in a difficult circumstance, which control could exhibit itself by delaying an action; to be slow towards, be long-enduring; to exercise patience, be long-suffering, clement, or indulgent, to forbear, Mt. 18:26, 29; 1Cor. 13:4; 1Thess. 5:14; 2Pet. 3:9; to have patience, endure patiently, wait with patient expectation, Heb. 6:15; Jas. 5:7, 8; to bear long with entreaties for deliverance and avengement, Lk. 18:7.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: makro-thumeo, to be long-suffering, epi tini towards one, [LXX+NT]; 2 to be slow to help, [NT]; 3 persevere, [Refs 1st c.AD+]; 4 with accusative, bear patiently, [LXX] bear up! [Refs 2nd c.AD+]

term_inventory_greek.meaning: perseverance, endurance, patience; patient endurance, 2Cor. 12:12; Col. 1:11; patient awaiting, Lk. 21:19; a patient frame of mind, patience, Rom. 5:3, 4; 15:4, 5; Jas. 1:3; perseverance, endurance, Rom. 2:7; endurance in adherence to an object, 1Thess. 1:3; 2Thess. 3:5; Rev. 1:9; constantly, perseveringly, Lk. 8:15; Rom. 8:25; Heb. 12:1; an enduring of affliction, the act of suffering, undergoing, 2Cor. 1:6; 6:4.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: hupomonē, he, remaining behind [Refs 4th c.BC+]; II endurance of involuntary toils [Refs 5th c.BC+]; the sword's power to sustain blows [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; II.2 in bad sense, obstinacy [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; II.3 of plants, power to endure [Refs 4th c.BC+]; III enduring to do shameful works [Refs]

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to stay behind; to stand firm, endure, persevere; intrans. to remain or stay behind, when others have departed, Lk. 2:43; transitive to bear up under, endure, suffer patiently, 1Cor. 13:7; Heb. 10:32; absolute to continue firmly, hold out, remain constant, persevere, Mt. 10:22; 24:13.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: hupomenō, stay behind [Refs 8th c.BC+]; remain alive [Refs 5th c.BC+]; of things, to be left behind [Refs 2nd c.AD+]; to be permanent [Refs 4th c.BC+]; II.1 abide or await another [Refs 5th c.BC+]; II.2 to be patient under, abide patiently, submit to any evil that threatens one [Refs 5th c.BC+]; II.3 stand one's ground, stand firm [Refs 8th c.BC+]; II.4 submit, bear, or dare to do a thing [Refs 8th c.BC+]; II.5-10 extended senses of patient endurance and submission [Refs 5th c.BC+ through 6th c.AD+]

cross_registry_links.note: aph (H0639) is the primary anger term in the OT and is the constituent element of the compound arek aph (slow to anger). The patience word set necessarily includes the full anger term set because patience is defined in OT as the restraint or lengthening of anger.
ya.chal (H3176) carries both the waiting and hoping senses and will likely overlap with any hope word set. The related word to.che.let (hope) is a derivative.

Eph 4:2 appears in both the humility word set (tapeinofrosunē) and the patience word set (makrothumia): 'with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love.' Col 3:12 similarly co-lists humility and patience.

la.ah (H3811) meaning 'to be weary, impatient' is the negative of patience and is semantically contrastive with the ARK root family. Jer 15:6 attributes la.ah to God ('I am weary of relenting'), creating a parallel with the god_as_subject instances in the ARK family.

data_quality_flags.description: The registry header lists the compound term 'arek aph' (slow to anger) with the note that a STEP search for this compound returned no results. No word analysis block exists for this compound. The concept is covered by its component terms a.rekh (H0750) and aph (H0639), which are both present with full word analysis blocks.

aph is stated as occurring about 278 times. The verse set recorded in the source file contains 219 verses. The majority of the verse set represents anger/wrath occurrences unrelated to the patience sense. The patience-relevant subset is confined to the 'slow to anger' (arek aph) compound occurrences — approximately 12 verses including Exo 34:6, Num 14:18, Psa 86:15, Psa 103:8, Pro 14:29, Pro 15:18, Pro 16:32, Nah 1:3, Joe 2:13, Jon 4:2, Neh 9:17, Psa 145:8. All 219 verse records are included per spec; the patience-relevant subset should be identified in Session B.

The dominant sense across the verse set is physical measurement (tabernacle, temple, Ezekiel's vision dimensions). Meaning definition 2 — forbearance, self-restraint (of patience) — has only one clear patience-relevant verse in the entire recorded set: Pro 25:15 ('With patience a ruler may be persuaded, and a soft tongue will break a bone'). All 90 verse records are included per spec.
